summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (Python 3.11, Linux Consumption) that calls GPT-4o via TRAPI using managed identity to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster and persists the result to an Azure Storage Blob container. No API keys are permitted; all auth must use Entra ID managed identity and RBAC.
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the GitHub/Azure OpenAI Token-based REST API, accessed via Entra ID managed identity (no static API keys).
Python 3.11 is the implementation language, consistent with existing infra (linuxFxVersion: Python|3.11).
Azure Functions Consumption plan (Linux) is the deployment target, consistent with existing Bicep infra.
The blob container 'yankees-roster' already exists or will be created by the Bicep infra module.
GitHub Actions is the CI/CD pipeline, consistent with the .github/workflows directory.
Bicep is the IaC toolchain, consistent with existing /infra directory and repository memories.
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName), not connection string.
Nightly schedule is UTC midnight (cron: '0 0 * * *') unless specified otherwise.
The Function App's system-assigned managed identity will be used for both Storage and TRAPI access.
Output blob will be a JSON file (e.g., yankees-roster.json) written to the 'yankees-roster' container.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and required Entra ID scope/audience for token acquisition?
Should the nightly run overwrite a single blob or append/version each run (e.g., timestamped filenames)?
Are there specific performance SLAs or timeout requirements for the GPT-4o call?
What is the target Azure subscription/resource group and region for deployment?
Should failed runs trigger an alert notification (email, Teams, PagerDuty)?
Is a specific GPT-4o model version or deployment name required for the TRAPI call?
What RBAC role is required on the TRAPI resource for managed identity access?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all required Azure infrastructure, CI/CD pipeline, and managed identity configuration to unblock application development.
entry_criteria: Task plan reviewed and approved (PRJ-01 complete)
Azure subscription and resource group available
exit_criteria: Function App, Storage Account, and blob container provisioned via Bicep
Managed identity assigned with required RBAC roles for Storage and TRAPI
GitHub Actions workflow deploys successfully to Function App
All Foundation PRs merged and reviewed by @copilot

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the timer-triggered Azure Function that retrieves the 1985 Yankees roster from GPT-4o via TRAPI and writes it to Azure Blob Storage.
entry_criteria: All Foundation wave exit criteria met
Managed identity RBAC roles confirmed active
exit_criteria: Timer trigger fires on nightly cron schedule
TRAPI GPT-4o call returns full 1985 Yankees roster
Roster JSON written to 'yankees-roster' blob container
Unit tests passing for all app modules
All Core Build PRs merged and reviewed by @copilot

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add retry/error handling, enforce no-API-key security policy, and configure structured logging and alerting in Application Insights.
entry_criteria: All Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function executing end-to-end without errors in dev/staging
exit_criteria: TRAPI and blob operations retry on transient failures
No static credentials or API keys present in code or config
Structured logs emitted to Application Insights with correlation IDs
Alert rule fires on function failure
All Hardening PRs merged and reviewed by @copilot

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end acceptance criteria, deploy to production, and complete operational documentation.
entry_criteria: All Hardening wave exit criteria met
No open critical or major defects
exit_criteria: End-to-end integration test passes in production
Nightly execution confirmed with blob containing full 1985 Yankees roster
All success criteria in spec.md checked off

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this implementation task plan for completeness, sequencing, and alignment with spec.md requirements. Approve to unblock Foundation wave work.
outputs: GitHub issues created for each task
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by project owner (rciapala) and engineering lead
All open questions triaged and assigned for resolution
GitHub issues created from task plan with correct labels and assignments
Plan approved and Foundation wave work unblocked
risks: Open questions about TRAPI endpoint and auth scope could block INF-03 and APP-02
Unclear blob versioning strategy could affect APP-03 design

tasks.title: Provision Azure Function App, Storage, and blob container via Bicep
tasks.description: Author or update Bicep modules under /infra to provision the Linux Consumption Function App (Python 3.11), Storage Account, and 'yankees-roster' blob container. Use identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (no listKeys). Tag resources project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala.
outputs: Deployed Function App, Storage Account, blob container
acceptance_criteria: Bicep deployment completes without errors (`az deployment group create` exits 0)
Function App exists with kind=functionapp,linux, reserved=true, linuxFxVersion=Python|3.11
Storage Account created with blob container named 'yankees-roster'
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName app setting present (no connection string with key)
Resources tagged with project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
Deployment validated via `az functionapp show` and `az storage container exists`
risks: Existing Bicep modules may need refactoring if resource names conflict
Consumption plan cold-start latency for nightly job is acceptable but must be verified

tasks.title: Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD deployment pipeline
tasks.description: Create or update GitHub Actions workflow(s) under .github/workflows to lint, test, and deploy the Python Azure Function to the provisioned Function App using OIDC/federated identity (no stored credentials). Include a deployment slot or manual approval gate for production.
inputs: Function App name from INF-01 outputs
outputs: Successful CI/CD pipeline run deploying to Function App
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to main and on pull_request
Pipeline runs Python linting (flake8/ruff) and unit tests before deploy
Deployment uses Azure OIDC federated identity (no stored service principal secret)
Successful deployment verified via `az functionapp show --query state` returning 'Running'
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
Pipeline run log link included in PR description
risks: OIDC federated identity requires Azure AD app registration and GitHub environment configuration
Consumption plan deployment may time out on first cold deploy

tasks.title: Assign managed identity RBAC roles for Storage and TRAPI access
tasks.description: Update /infra Bicep RBAC module to assign the Function App's system-assigned managed identity the 'Storage Blob Data Owner' role on the Storage Account and the required role on the TRAPI resource. No API keys or connection strings should be used.
inputs: INF-01 outputs (Function App principal ID, Storage Account ID)
TRAPI resource ID and required role (from open question resolution)
outputs: Role assignments confirmed in Azure portal/CLI
acceptance_criteria: Function App system-assigned managed identity has 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' or 'Storage Blob Data Owner' on the Storage Account
Function App managed identity has required RBAC role on TRAPI resource
Role assignments verified via `az role assignment list --assignee <principalId>`
No API keys, SAS tokens, or connection strings present in app settings
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
Bicep deployment re-run with RBAC module update exits 0
risks: TRAPI role assignment details are an open question; may block APP-02
RBAC propagation delay (up to 5 minutes) can cause transient auth failures in testing

tasks.title: Implement nightly timer-triggered Azure Function scaffold
tasks.description: Create the Azure Function project structure with a TimerTrigger function configured for nightly execution (cron: '0 0 * * *' UTC). Include function.json or decorator-based trigger config, requirements.txt with azure-functions and azure-identity, and host.json with appropriate timeout and extension bundle settings.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model docs
acceptance_criteria: Function project structure is valid and passes `func start` locally
TimerTrigger cron expression '0 0 * * *' verified with unit test asserting schedule fires at midnight UTC
Unit tests for timer trigger scaffold pass: `pytest tests/ -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
host.json sets functionTimeout to at least 5 minutes
requirements.txt includes azure-functions>=1.17, azure-identity>=1.15, azure-storage-blob>=12.19
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
risks: Python v2 programming model vs v1 model choice must be consistent across all functions
Nightly cron timezone must be confirmed (UTC assumed)

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI GPT-4o integration to retrieve 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that authenticates to TRAPI using DefaultAzureCredential (managed identity, no API keys), calls the GPT-4o endpoint with a prompt to list all members of the 1985 New York Yankees, and returns a structured list of player names. Include unit tests with mocked TRAPI responses.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and Entra ID scope (from PRJ-01 open question resolution)
outputs: Unit tests with mocked HTTP responses
acceptance_criteria: DefaultAzureCredential used for token acquisition (no hardcoded API keys or secrets)
GPT-4o response parsed into a list of at least 25 players (1985 Yankees full roster)
Unit tests mock TRAPI HTTP calls and assert correct parsing: `pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
Module raises a typed exception on TRAPI call failure (non-2xx response)
No credentials, tokens, or API keys present in source code or config files
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
risks: TRAPI endpoint and auth scope are open questions; work cannot begin until resolved
GPT-4o response format may vary; prompt engineering needed for consistent structured output
Managed identity not available in local dev; local testing requires Azure CLI credential fallback

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage write for players roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that uses DefaultAzureCredential and BlobServiceClient to write the players list as a JSON blob to the 'yankees-roster' container in Azure Storage. The blob should be named 'yankees-roster.json' (or timestamped per open question resolution). Include unit tests with mocked BlobServiceClient.
inputs: Storage account name from INF-01 outputs
acceptance_criteria: BlobServiceClient authenticated via DefaultAzureCredential (no connection string with account key)
Players list serialized as UTF-8 JSON and written to 'yankees-roster' container
Blob overwrite behavior confirmed (or versioning strategy documented per open question)
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient.upload_blob and assert correct JSON payload: `pytest tests/test_blob_storage.py -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
Module raises a typed exception on blob write failure
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
risks: Identity-based storage auth requires RBAC propagation; integration tests may fail transiently
Blob overwrite vs versioning strategy unresolved (open question)

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights and wire to Function App
tasks.description: Add an Application Insights resource to the Bicep infra and configure the Function App with APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting (connection-string-based, not instrumentation key). Ensure distributed tracing and live metrics are enabled.
outputs: Function App emitting telemetry to Application Insights
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights resource provisioned via Bicep (`az monitor app-insights component show` succeeds)
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting present in Function App (not APPINSIGHTS_INSTRUMENTATIONKEY)
Function execution telemetry visible in Application Insights Live Metrics within 5 minutes of a manual trigger
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
Bicep deployment re-run exits 0 with Application Insights output
risks: Using deprecated instrumentation key instead of connection string reduces observability capability
Application Insights ingestion latency may delay alert detection

tasks.title: Enforce no-API-key policy and validate managed identity authentication
tasks.description: Perform a security review to confirm no static API keys, SAS tokens, or connection strings with account keys exist in code, app settings, or Key Vault references. Validate that DefaultAzureCredential is the sole auth mechanism for TRAPI and Storage. Run a secrets scan (e.g., gitleaks) in CI.
outputs: Secrets scan integrated in CI pipeline
Confirmed zero static credentials in repo or config
acceptance_criteria: gitleaks or equivalent secrets scanner runs in GitHub Actions and exits 0 on main branch
No API keys, account keys, SAS tokens, or passwords found in source code, app settings, or environment variables
TRAPI authentication confirmed to use Entra ID token via DefaultAzureCredential (verified in logs)
Storage authentication confirmed to use managed identity (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName present, no AzureWebJobsStorage connection string)
Security review sign-off documented in PR
risks: Developer workstations may cache Azure CLI credentials that mask managed identity failures in local testing
TRAPI token scope misconfiguration could cause silent auth failures in production

tasks.title: Implement retry logic and error handling for TRAPI and Blob operations
tasks.description: Add retry logic (exponential backoff, max 3 attempts) to the TRAPI client and blob storage module for transient failures (HTTP 429, 503, network timeouts). Implement structured error handling that logs failures with context and prevents silent data loss. Include unit tests for retry behavior.
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_storage.py with retry logic
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff on HTTP 429 and 503
Blob write retries up to 3 times on transient network errors
Unit tests simulate transient failures and assert retry count: `pytest tests/test_retry.py -v` exits 0 (link test results in PR)
All exceptions are caught at the function entry point and logged with structured context (run ID, timestamp, error type)
Function does not silently swallow exceptions that prevent roster from being written
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged before proceeding
risks: Aggressive retry logic on TRAPI could exhaust token quotas
Timer function has a max execution timeout; retries must complete within host.json functionTimeout

tasks.title: Configure structured logging, metrics, and alerting in Application Insights
tasks.description: Add structured logging via Python logging module with Application Insights handler to emit run ID, player count, blob write status, and duration metrics. Create an Azure Monitor alert rule that fires when the function fails or does not execute within the expected nightly window.
outputs: Structured log events in Application Insights
Alert rule firing on function failure or missed execution
acceptance_criteria: Function emits structured log events with fields: run_id, player_count, blob_name, duration_ms, status
Application Insights query `traces | where message contains 'yankees-roster'` returns results after a test run
Azure Monitor alert rule configured to fire when function execution count drops to 0 over a 25-hour window
Alert rule verified by manually disabling the function and confirming alert fires within the evaluation period
Log retention set to at least 30 days in Application Insights
risks: Alert rule evaluation window must account for Consumption plan cold start and execution jitter
Application Insights sampling could drop structured log events at high volume (not applicable here but configure to 100%)

tasks.title: Execute end-to-end integration test and validate acceptance criteria
tasks.description: Execute a full end-to-end integration test in the production environment: trigger the function manually, verify the TRAPI call succeeds, confirm the 'yankees-roster.json' blob is written with a complete 1985 Yankees roster (at least 25 players), and validate all spec acceptance criteria are met.
outputs: Confirmed blob content with full 1985 Yankees roster
All spec acceptance criteria checked off
acceptance_criteria: Manual function trigger via Azure portal or `func azure functionapp publish` succeeds
Blob 'yankees-roster.json' in container 'yankees-roster' contains at least 25 player entries
Blob content validated with `az storage blob download` and JSON parsed successfully
Application Insights shows successful execution trace with player_count >= 25
Nightly timer trigger confirmed active (next execution scheduled) via Azure portal Function Monitor
All spec.md functional acceptance criteria checked off and documented in QA report
risks: GPT-4o response may be non-deterministic; roster completeness check requires known ground truth list
Production RBAC propagation may still be in progress on first test run

tasks.title: Validate nightly timer trigger in production and confirm first scheduled execution
tasks.description: Confirm the nightly timer trigger is active and scheduled in production, observe the first autonomous nightly execution, and validate the resulting blob output. Ensure the Function App is not stopped or disabled and that no app setting overrides the schedule.
outputs: Blob written autonomously without manual trigger
acceptance_criteria: Function App is in 'Running' state and not manually disabled
Timer trigger next execution time shown in Azure portal Function Monitor matches expected nightly schedule
First autonomous nightly execution log visible in Application Insights within 24 hours of deployment
Blob 'yankees-roster.json' updated by the autonomous execution (last-modified timestamp matches execution time)
No AzureWebJobsDisableHomepage or FUNCTIONS_WORKER_PROCESS_COUNT misconfig present
risks: Consumption plan may not wake the function if there is no activity; ensure AzureWebJobsStorage is correctly configured
First nightly execution may fail due to RBAC propagation; monitor Application Insights alerts

tasks.title: Write deployment and operational runbook
tasks.description: Author a README or runbook documenting: repository structure, local development setup, deployment steps, environment variables, TRAPI auth configuration, blob storage schema, monitoring queries, and escalation path for nightly execution failures.
outputs: README.md or docs/runbook.md with complete operational documentation
acceptance_criteria: README includes: project overview, architecture diagram or description, local dev setup steps, and deployment instructions
Runbook documents how to manually trigger the function and validate blob output
Runbook includes Application Insights Kusto queries for common failure scenarios
Runbook documents escalation path if nightly execution fails
Documentation reviewed and approved by project owner (rciapala)
risks: Documentation may become stale if written before all implementation decisions are finalized
